entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Subject of genetic analysis for sleep and circadian rhythm traits

entities.name: Chronotype
entities.context: Sleep timing preference; V has intermediate chronotype with flexibility

entities.name: Chronotype Flexibility
entities.context: Ability to train sleep schedule; V possesses this due to lack of extreme genetic pull

entities.name: Adenosine Sensitivity
entities.context: How body responds to adenosine buildup; V has standard sensitivity

entities.name: Circadian Cycling
entities.context: Biological sleep-wake rhythm; V has standard cycling patterns

entities.name: CLOCK Gene (rs1801260)
entities.context: Genetic marker determining chronotype; V's AG variant indicates intermediate

entities.name: ADORA2A Gene (rs5751876)
entities.context: Genetic marker for adenosine/caffeine response; V's TT variant is standard

entities.name: PER2 Gene (rs228697)
entities.context: Genetic marker for circadian rhythm; V's CC variant indicates standard cycling

entities.name: Consistent Wake Time
entities.context: Behavioral practice especially important for those with chronotype flexibility

entities.name: Caffeine
entities.context: Substance that targets adenosine receptors; relevant to ADORA2A gene

relationships.context: V's genetics indicate trainable sleep schedule rather than hardwired pattern
CC genotype indicates standard circadian patterns
Caffeine targets adenosine receptors governed by this gene
Key behavioral lever for those without strong genetic chronotype
Coaching implication: more important for V than for those with fixed chronotype
